fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 49-year-old African American male with schizophrenia with residual symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Williams attended appointment with case manager. Case manager confirms he takes olanzapine 10 mg nightly via supervised administration at program, no missed doses. Despite reliable adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voices discussing him. Endorses vague paranoid ideas. Sleep disrupted at 5-6 hours nightly. Appetite fair, weight stable at 182 lbs. Attends day program three days weekly. Not working, receives disability. Lives in group home. Limited social interaction. Mood 'okay' rated 5/10. Denies substance use. Flat during interview. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Patricia Johnson, LMSW, Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged male, adequate grooming, appears withdrawn

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives in group home; unemployed, on disability; minimal engagement; case management services.
Assessment_and_Plan: Will increase olanzapine to 15 mg qHS.
fields.Author: Note written by Patricia Johnson, LMSW, Social Worker — Community Care Homes, 5623 Group Living Way, Cleveland, OH 44102, Phone: (555) 447-8821, Fax: (555) 447-8822
